title: host-side allowlist gates plugin-supplied factory prefixes
rationale: Plugins ship their own allowed_prefixes inside .plugin/vigor.json. Without a host gate, any plugin author could declare allowed_prefixes=['os'] and import arbitrary modules, defeating the per-FactoryRef namespace assertion. AgentConfig now carries allowed_plugin_factory_prefixes (default empty = 'no plugins'); adapter_spec_from_plugin enforces a dotted-component match between each plugin-supplied prefix and the host allowlist before the spec is built.

name: agent-demo-mcp-fake-session-opener
description: Test agent configs with declared MCP servers by constructing MCPToolBackend(specs, session_opener=fake) and injecting via AgentOrchestrator(cfg, tool_backend=...). MCPToolBackend.session_opener is the documented injection point so CI never spawns subprocesses. Pattern used in packages/vigor-agent/tests/test_e2e_demo.py to exercise the same config operators run in production.

ManimRenderConfig with manim_exe=None raises 'Manim executable not found on PATH' from build_command BEFORE the injected runner sees the cmd, because shutil.which('manim') is consulted first. Result: even with a fake Runner, the adapter compile path returns a render_error and the run fails.
resolution: Set ManimRenderConfig(manim_exe='/usr/bin/env') (or any path that satisfies build_command's existence check) when injecting a fake runner; the runner ignores the executable in cmd[0] and just synthesizes the expected output file at expected_output_path(...).
